title: SSRF Attack Detected Against Internal Metadata Service

steps.message: Block the source IP address and any detected malicious URL patterns in the WAF/firewall to prevent further outbound connections and internal network scanning, as part of containing likely malicious intent. The application should integrate its defenses against such attacks with logging and alerting.

Terminate the user's session and suspend the associated account, if applicable, based on the detection of malicious intent to access internal resources, consistent with actions for an attempted security breach.

Query the SIEM for any logs related to the access of 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/', and investigate for further attempts to connect to arbitrary servers/ports or other services on the loopback address, following the HACK STEPS for SSRF exploitation (5.a, 5.c).

Initiate a code review to identify the specific request parameters that allow the application to connect to hostnames, IP addresses, or full URLs, as per SSRF HACK STEPS (1 & 5). Implement robust input validation and sanitization for these parameters.

params.summary: Code Review: SSRF vulnerability allowing metadata access
params.description: Investigate application code for parameters enabling SSRF to internal services (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) and apply input validation/sanitization to prevent arbitrary connections.

steps.message: Approve the deployment of the code fix to remediate the SSRF vulnerability. Ensure the fix properly implements input validation for all parameters capable of specifying hostnames, IP addresses, or full URLs, which is a critical step in preventing exploitation.

Deploy the approved code fix to the affected application. This fix must implement robust input validation for all parameters that could facilitate connections to arbitrary URLs, as outlined in the SSRF HACK STEPS.

Send a notification to the #security-incidents Slack channel confirming the SSRF incident targeting the internal metadata service has been contained and remediated, and initiate a post-mortem review task.

params.summary: SSRF to metadata endpoint contained and patched. Root cause analysis initiated.
